description_long: This is a vintage biplane with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other, both featuring a light blue color with yellow edging. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the tail, with a white nose and a visible wooden propeller mounted at the front. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both matching the wing color scheme. The landing gear consists of two large, fixed wheels at the front and a small tail skid at the rear. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The structure is supported by a network of struts and wires, typical of early 20th-century aircraft construction.
description: A vintage blue and yellow biplane with an open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear. The aircraft features two stacked wings and a classic tail design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, white nose, wooden propeller, and large fixed wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stacked wing structure, open cockpit, and the tail's vertical and horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, struts, and the alignment of the wings and landing gear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wing configuration, cockpit placement, and tail skid.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings; no winglets; slight dihedral angle on upper wing.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
